Title: Auxin-salicylic acid seesaw regulates the age-dependent balance between plant growth and herbivore defense

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: mediates anti-whitefly defense; broader plant defense hormone
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Negatively regulates salicylic acid accumulation and SA-mediated whitefly defense by repressing NbARF18La/b transcripts.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbmiR160c is more abundant in juvenile plants and is induced by auxin. 2. NbmiR160c directly cleaves NbARF18La and NbARF18Lb transcripts at predicted coding-region target sites, validated by degradome sequencing and sensor assays. 3. Overexpression of NbmiR160c lowers NbMYB42 and NbPAL6 expression, reduces SA accumulation, and weakens whitefly resistance, whereas target mimic inhibition has the opposite effect.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: miRNA overexpression; target mimic inhibition; degradome sequencing; GFP/GUS/LUC sensor assays; RT-qPCR; metabolite profiling; insect bioassay
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in 21-day-old juvenile plants than in 35-day-old adult plants; induced by IAA
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher in juvenile leaves; induced by auxin treatment
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: plant age; whitefly infestation context; exogenous IAA treatment; mimic160c expression
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: NbARF18La; NbARF18Lb; AGO1 (proposed in model figure)
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S or constitutive transient-expression vectors for effectors; native pNbmiR160c promoter reporters
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: By repressing SA defense, favors early growth during juvenile stage; inhibition of NbmiR160c enhances defense but may strengthen growth-defense tradeoff.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: miRNA-seq (21-day-old plants, accession NA); degradome sequencing (accession NA)
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target mimic or down-regulation of NbmiR160c could elevate SA-based insect resistance, but likely requires age- or tissue-specific tuning to avoid growth penalties from excessive SA.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Functions posttranscriptionally rather than as protein-coding gene; auxin activates its transcription.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: mediates anti-whitefly defense; broader defense hormone
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Depletes salicylic acid, abolishing age-related SA accumulation and reducing whitefly resistance.
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NahG transgenic plants fail to accumulate sufficient SA with age or after whitefly infestation. 2. SA-marker genes are reduced in NahG plants. 3. Whiteflies perform better on NahG plants, and exogenous SA rescues resistance.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: transgenic expression; SA quantification; marker gene expression; insect bioassay; chemical complementation with SA
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: plant age; whitefly infestation; exogenous SA treatment
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced defense; in discussion, low SA is associated with enhanced auxin-gene expression.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a functional tool to demonstrate SA dependence, but not a breeding target for improving nutrient-related defense metabolite accumulation.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Heterologous bacterial gene used to deplete SA; included because it is experimentally central to metabolite causality.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: salicylic acid biosynthesis via the PAL pathway
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: PAL branch of SA biosynthesis; phenylpropanoid-associated SA route
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Deaminates L-phenylalanine to trans-cinnamic acid, supplying the PAL-mediated route to salicylic acid biosynthesis.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point supported in this paper for age-related SA accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: mediates anti-whitefly defense; broader plant immunity hormone
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes age-related salicylic acid accumulation and SA-mediated resistance to whiteflies.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Among tested NbPAL genes, only NbPAL6 is up-regulated with age. 2. Silencing NbPAL6 lowers SA and NbPR1 levels and compromises whitefly resistance in adult plants. 3. Overexpressing NbPAL6 in juvenile plants increases SA and defense, showing that NbPAL6 is a major driver of age-related SA-mediated resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: VIGS knockdown; transient overexpression; RT-qPCR; SA quantification; insect bioassay
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in 35-day-old adult plants than in 21-day-old juvenile plants
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Age-upregulated in leaves; repressed by auxin; activated by NbMYB42
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: plant age; whitefly infestation context; exogenous IAA; auxin-biosynthesis inhibitors
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S for overexpression; native pNbPAL6 promoter in GUS/LUC reporters
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Elevated SA-mediated defense can impair early growth through auxin antagonism when SA is excessive.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A promising engineering target to raise SA-based insect resistance, but deployment should be developmentally tuned to avoid juvenile growth inhibition from excessive SA.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Paper notes NbPAL6 may not be the sole PAL contributing to SA synthesis; other PALs may contribute redundantly under other conditions.

Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates NbPAL6 transcription by directly binding MYB cis-elements in the NbPAL6 promoter.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Essential age-related activator linking developmental cues to PAL-mediated SA accumulation and whitefly defense.
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: NbARF18La; NbARF18Lb; auxin negatively affects its transcript abundance indirectly via NbARF repression; plant age
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Activates SA accumulation and enhances whitefly resistance by inducing NbPAL6.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbMYB42 binds the NbPAL6 promoter, with ChIP-qPCR and EMSA supporting direct interaction. 2. Overexpression of NbMYB42 strongly elevates NbPAL6, SA, and defense in juvenile plants. 3. Silencing NbMYB42 decreases NbPAL6, SA, and whitefly resistance in adult plants.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; nuclear localization; GUS and dual-luciferase transactivation assays; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; overexpression; VIGS; SA quantification; insect bioassay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in 35-day-old adult plants than in 21-day-old juvenile plants
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Age-upregulated in leaves; reduced by auxin treatment
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: plant age; exogenous IAA; whitefly bioassay context
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S for effector expression; native pNbPAL6 promoter and pNbMYB42 promoter reporters
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Activation of SA biosynthesis may enhance defense at the cost of early growth when SA becomes excessive.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful positive regulator for boosting SA-mediated insect resistance, especially with inducible or age-specific expression to minimize growth penalties.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Previously implicated in lignin synthesis and whitefly resistance in N. benthamiana, indicating broader phenylpropanoid-defense roles.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Auxin response factor with B3 DNA-binding domain and C-terminal dimerization domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation; posttranscriptional repression by miRNA
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional control of salicylic acid biosynthesis via NbMYB42-NbPAL6 module
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates NbMYB42 transcription by direct binding to the P2 AuxRE region in the NbMYB42 promoter.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key age-dependent upstream activator of the NbMYB42-NbPAL6 module; loss reduces SA accumulation and whitefly defense.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of auxin-to-SA antagonistic feedforward repression circuit via auxin-induced NbmiR160c
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SA accumulation and whitefly resistance through activation of NbMYB42 and downstream NbPAL6.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbARF18La directly binds the NbMYB42 promoter and activates its transcription. 2. Overexpression raises NbMYB42, NbPAL6, NbPR1 and enhances whitefly resistance; silencing lowers SA and defense. 3. Its mRNA is repressed posttranscriptionally by auxin-induced NbmiR160c, especially in juvenile plants.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; subcellular localization; GUS and dual-luciferase assays; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; overexpression; VIGS; Y2H; BiFC; pull-down; RT-qPCR; SA quantification; insect bioassay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in 35-day-old adult plants than in 21-day-old juvenile plants
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Age-upregulated; reduced by IAA; increased by auxin inhibitors; targeted by NbmiR160c
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: plant age; exogenous IAA/NAA; L-Kyn; Yucasin
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S for effector expression; native pNbMYB42 promoter reporters
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Activation of downstream SA defense may contribute to growth-defense tradeoff in juvenile plants if derepressed too early.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: degradome sequencing for miRNA-guided cleavage support, accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A candidate to elevate constitutive insect defense by strengthening the SA module, but should be combined with age-specific control or miRNA-site editing to limit growth costs.

Regulation_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Auxin response factor with B3 DNA-binding domain and C-terminal dimerization domain
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: transcriptional activation; posttranscriptional repression by miRNA
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: transcriptional control of salicylic acid biosynthesis via NbMYB42-NbPAL6 module
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates NbMYB42 transcription by direct binding to the P2 AuxRE region in the NbMYB42 promoter.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Key age-dependent upstream activator of the NbMYB42-NbPAL6 module; contributes to SA accumulation and whitefly defense.
Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: Part of auxin-to-SA antagonistic feedforward repression circuit via auxin-induced NbmiR160c
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes SA accumulation and whitefly resistance through activation of NbMYB42 and downstream NbPAL6.
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NbARF18Lb directly binds and activates the NbMYB42 promoter. 2. Overexpression enhances downstream SA-defense gene expression and resistance, whereas silencing reduces SA and defense. 3. NbmiR160c directly cleaves NbARF18Lb mRNA, linking auxin to repression of the SA pathway in juvenile plants.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Y1H; subcellular localization; GUS and dual-luciferase assays; ChIP-qPCR; EMSA; overexpression; VIGS; Y2H/BiFC/pull-down with NbARF18La; RT-qPCR; SA quantification; insect bioassay
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Higher in 35-day-old adult plants than in 21-day-old juvenile plants
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Age-upregulated; reduced by IAA; increased by auxin inhibitors; targeted by NbmiR160c
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: plant age; exogenous IAA/NAA; L-Kyn; Yucasin
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: 35S for effector expression; native pNbMYB42 promoter reporters
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely contributes to growth-defense tradeoff when SA pathway is activated prematurely.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: degradome sequencing for miRNA-guided cleavage support, accession NA
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential engineering target for stronger whitefly resistance; miRNA-resistant alleles or stage-specific activation could be useful while minimizing juvenile growth penalties.
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Phylogenetically related to Arabidopsis ARF16/18-like clade as described by authors.
